clinical_history: 69-year-old male with coronary artery disease and  a history of prior myocardial infarction.  What is the rhythm accounting for his irregular pulse noted after "coronary artery bypass graft" surgery?
diagnosis: d)  Note the longer PR in the atrial premature complexes. There is also evidence of underlying anterior and inferior Q-wave myocardial infarctions (MIs).  Left atrial abnormality (LAA) is noted in the sinus beats.   Frequent atrial ectopy, especially in the post "coronary artery bypass graft (CABG) surgery" setting, may be a forerunner of atrial fibrillation.
